page: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, this server allocates shared CPU resources with 1.0 thread per core. It is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 1000 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 99610.25 and a PassMark CPU score of 64051.5, both placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance is highly competitive, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance reaching 3132.38, placing it in the top 10%. Memory subsystem performance is a notable highlight; the PassMark memory score of 3040.25 ranks in the top 10%, supported by a read-only small block cached memory bandwidth of 4812.14 GB/sec. For database and key-value operations, the server achieves 76.91 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, while static web serving throughput averages 31.35 GB/sec.

The server's hardware profile presents a qualitative tradeoff between high compute density and memory capacity, offering a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio that favors processing power over massive memory footprints. The shared CPU allocation model may introduce performance variability compared to dedicated instances, though the high core count and integrated NVMe storage provide a cost-efficient resource density for parallel processing. This configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded decompression tasks, medium-scale database operations, and lightweight LLM inference workloads, as demonstrated by its top-tier text generation speed of 43.67 tokens/second on medium-sized models.
description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 32 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC Milan processor at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features a shared CPU allocation with 32 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. It includes a bundled 1000 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores both reaching the top 10% tier. The server delivers 76.91 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and demonstrates efficient multi-threaded decompression. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server represents a cost-efficient option for compute-heavy workloads, parallel processing, and medium-scale database operations that require high core density and fast local storage without requiring massive memory allocations.

64.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
